framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Public chemistry science communication via a self-produced video channel
paths.what_it_is: Building a public YouTube/TikTok channel where a trained chemist runs and films real syntheses and reactions, explains the underlying chemistry in depth, and grows a large recurring audience around it.
paths.why_it_signals: Sustaining a channel like this demands real bench mastery performed under public scrutiny plus the discipline to explain and package it consistently, which reads as initiative and communication skill worth sponsoring.

paths.what_it_is: Contributing merged, production-grade features or documentation to widely used open-source chemistry software such as RDKit, or shipping and publicly writing up your own cheminformatics tools.
paths.why_it_signals: A merged contribution to software the whole industry runs on is public, verifiable proof of both chemistry judgment and software craft that a resume line can't fake.

paths.what_it_is: Running a long-running blog or newsletter that translates real pharma or chemical-industry work, technical judgment, and trends for a broad technical readership.
paths.why_it_signals: Sustained, well-argued public writing about the industry demonstrates deep domain fluency plus the rarer ability to communicate it clearly, a combination recruiters treat as a proxy for seniority beyond years of experience.
